The reason for this logic being in service layer is because the persistence layer already implements getDataNodes() which was originally supposed to implement this logic but due to the requirement of CPS-1422 check for Invalid xPath and non-existing xpath is not implemented there and was moved to the service layer. Once the checks are done it calls getDataNodes on line 174. This is for team reference and we can discuss where to exactly keep/move the code 😊

CpsPathUtil.getNormalizedXpath always expects the xPath to start with /, So it works when xPath is set to '/' or '/bookstore' but fails when empty string is passed as xpath, that is "" which is one of the positive scenarios for root node xpath. So because of this there is separate block for root xpath. If we include the same in CpsPathUtil.getNormalizedXpath then it would also need to support "" as xpath which would defeat its purpose to check invalid xpath eg. 'Invalid-Xpath'.

Ran the test cases and CpsPathUtil.getNormalizedXpath works with xpath as '/' but fails when xpatrh is set to ""

Kept this because of the CPS-1301 ticket, I guess they need to check if get API returns the module prefix or not and so I just saw the test for V1 and thought it would be needed here as well. So removing first and third "and" block should be fine but I think we should keep the second one. Need inputs.
Done
Done
Done. Discussed with Luke and decided to remove 1st and 3rd and block. Kept the second and block as it will be needed during testing of for CPS-1301 and removing this might affect it.

OK we'll leave that for now, but the block should be limited then to the creation of the target xpath, I woudl refactor it like so:

final String targetXpath = isRootXpath(xpath) ? xpath : CpsPathUtil.getNormalizedXpath(xpath)
Collection xPaths = Collections.singletonList(targetXpath) : 
final Collection<DataNode> dataNodes = getDataNodes(dataspaceName, anchorName, xPaths, fetchDescendantsOption);
